description: internal:rule= — internal self-loop preserves the inner engine (§51.0.O). <Playing rule=.Title internal:rule=.Playing> declares an internal self-transition. Drive inner to .Battle (#fight), then take the internal self-loop @appMode=.Playing (#selfloop): the composite does NOT exit/re-enter, so the inner engine's variant survives as .Battle (contrast the external round-trip in engine-external-transition-resets-rt, which resets inner to .Exploring). NOTE (harness-observability): a self-target write is ALSO a §51.0.F.1 self-write no-op, so this state-snapshot observable coincides with the no-op path — it is a necessary-not-sufficient witness of internal:rule= (a discriminating internal-vs-external observable would need an <onTransition>-fires-on-external-only side effect; flagged to PA).
rationale: §51.0.O an internal transition (internal:rule=) does NOT exit the composite; the inner engine's lifecycle is preserved (no re-init). The inner cell survives the internal self-loop as .Battle.
